{{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft}}'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft}} as the “{{ill|Creation|de|Schöpfung|ja|創造|zh|创造|qq=draft}} Festival” on {{ill|Ipip|de|Ipip|ja|イピップ|zh|伊皮普|lt=13. Epiphi|lt_ja=13. エピフィ|lt_de=13. Epiphi|lt_zh=13. 伊皮普|qq=draft}}, the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节}}, was celebrated in this special {{ill|Esna|de|Esna|ja|エスナ|zh|埃斯纳|qq=draft}} form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female {{ill|Counterpart|de|Pendant|ja|カウンターパート|zh|对应物|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the {{ill|Dendera|de|Dendera|ja|デンデラ|zh|丹德拉|qq=draft}} Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Neith (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のネイス|zh|埃及神话中的Neith|qq=draft}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のラー|zh|埃及神话中的Re|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Apophis|de|Apophis|ja|アポフィス|zh|阿波菲斯|qq=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|qq=draft}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|セイス|zh|塞斯|qq=draft}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft}}'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft}} as the “{{ill|Creation|de|Schöpfung|ja|創造|zh|创造|qq=draft}} Festival” on {{ill|Ipip|de|Ipip|ja|イピップ|zh|伊皮普|lt=13. Epiphi|lt_ja=13. エピフィ|lt_de=13. Epiphi|lt_zh=13. 伊皮普|qq=draft}}, the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节}}, was celebrated in this special {{ill|Esna|de|Esna|ja|エスナ|zh|埃斯纳|qq=draft}} form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female {{ill|Counterpart|de|Pendant|ja|カウンターパート|zh|对应物|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the {{ill|Dendera|de|Dendera|ja|デンデラ|zh|丹德拉|qq=draft}} Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Neith (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のネイス|zh|埃及神话中的Neith|qq=draft}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のラー|zh|埃及神话中的Re|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Apophis|de|Apophis|ja|アポフィス|zh|阿波菲斯|qq=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|qq=draft}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|セイス|zh|塞斯|qq=draft}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のラー|zh|埃及神话中的Re|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Apophis|de|Apophis|ja|アポフィス|zh|阿波菲斯|qq=draft}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|qq=draft}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|セイス|zh|塞斯|qq=draft}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Herodotus|qq=}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chnum|qq=}}'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=}} as the “{{ill|Creation|de|Schöpfung|ja|創造|zh|创造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Creation|qq=}} Festival” on {{ill|Ipip|de|Ipip|ja|イピップ|zh|伊皮普|lt=13. Epiphi|lt_ja=13. エピフィ|lt_de=13. Epiphi|lt_zh=13. 伊皮普|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipip|qq=}}, the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=}}, was celebrated in this special {{ill|Esna|de|Esna|ja|エスナ|zh|埃斯纳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna|qq=}} form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female {{ill|Counterpart|de|Pendant|ja|カウンターパート|zh|对应物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Counterpart|qq=}} of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the {{ill|Dendera|de|Dendera|ja|デンデラ|zh|丹德拉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dendera|qq=}} Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Chnum|de|Chnum|ja|クヌム|zh|克努姆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chnum|qq=}} and {{ill|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Neith (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のネイス|zh|埃及神话中的Neith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neith (Egyptian Mythology)|qq=}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|エジプト神話のラー|zh|埃及神话中的Re|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Re (Egyptian Mythology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Apophis|de|Apophis|ja|アポフィス|zh|阿波菲斯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apophis|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|de|Thot|ja|トート|zh|托特|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoth|qq=}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais|de|Sais|ja|セイス|zh|塞斯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sais|qq=}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|de|Herodot|ja|ヘロドトス|zh|希罗多德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Herodotus|qq=}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altägypten|ja|古代エジプト|zh|古埃及|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1473666]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). revision.text:
[[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1473666]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

[[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1473666]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==
==References== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Neith-Fest (Esna)|version=201349737|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[ja:ネイト祭]] [[de:Neith-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Schemu III]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1473666}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

revision.text:
[[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1473666]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]]'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “[[:en:Creation myth|Creation]] Festival” on [[:en:Epip|13. Epiphi]], the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special [[:en:Esna|Esna]] form at no other place of [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female [[:en:Pendant (art)|Counterpart]] of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the [[:en:Dendera|Dendera]] Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] and [[:en:Neith|Neith (Egyptian Mythology)]] in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian Mythology)]], [[:en:Apophis|Apophis]], as well as [[:en:Thoth|Thoth]] and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to [[:en:Sais, Egypt|Sais]], where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} [[:en:Herodotus|Herodotus]] reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

revision.text:
[[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1473666]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}}'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “{{ill|Creation myth|en|Creation myth|WD=Q215304|lt=Creation}} Festival” on {{ill|Epip|en|Epip|WD=Q1672224|lt=13. Epiphi}}, the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special {{ill|Esna|en|Esna|WD=Q626031|lt=Esna}} form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female {{ill|Pendant (art)|en|Pendant (art)|WD=Q591644|lt=Counterpart}} of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the {{ill|Dendera|en|Dendera|WD=Q29019|lt=Dendera}} Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}} and {{ill|Neith|en|Neith|WD=Q191815|lt=Neith (Egyptian Mythology)}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian Mythology)}}, {{ill|Apophis|en|Apophis|WD=Q190511|lt=Apophis}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|WD=Q146921|lt=Thoth}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais, Egypt|en|Sais, Egypt|WD=Q381038|lt=Sais}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==
