Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}} and {{ill|Neith|en|Neith|WD=Q191815|lt=Neith (Egyptian Mythology)}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian Mythology)}}, {{ill|Apophis|en|Apophis|WD=Q190511|lt=Apophis}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|WD=Q146921|lt=Thoth}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais, Egypt|en|Sais, Egypt|WD=Q381038|lt=Sais}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==

A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==
==References== {{translated page|de|Neith-Fest (Esna)|version=201349737|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Neith-Fest (Esna)]] [[ja:ネイト祭]] [[de:Neith-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1473666}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Schemu III]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Khnum Temple in Esna |NAME = O6-X1:O1-W9-E10-A40
Hut-Chenmu
''Ḥwt-H̱nmw''
''House of {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum|1=Khnum}}'' |IMAGE1 = Esna Temple 2004 by John Campana.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Khnum Temple of Esna }} The “'''Neith Festival'''”, which was solemnly celebrated in the {{ill|Temple of Esna|de|Tempel von Esna|ja|エスナの神殿|zh|埃斯纳神庙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Esna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the “{{ill|Creation myth|en|Creation myth|WD=Q215304|lt=Creation|1=Creation myth}} Festival” on {{ill|Epip|en|Epip|WD=Q1672224|lt=13. Epiphi|1=Epip}}, the third month of the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェム#Datierungen|zh|Schemu#Datierungen|lt=Shemu Season|lt_ja=シェムの季節|lt_de=Schemu-Jahreszeit|lt_zh=Schemu季节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shemu#Dating|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was celebrated in this special {{ill|Esna|en|Esna|WD=Q626031|lt=Esna|1=Esna}} form at no other place of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}}. The Neith Festival was regarded as the female {{ill|Pendant (art)|en|Pendant (art)|WD=Q591644|lt=Counterpart|1=Pendant (art)}} of the {{ill|Khnum Festival|de|Chnum-Fest|ja|クヌム祭|zh|克努姆节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khnum Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “{{ill|Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|de|Festsetzen der Töpferscheibe|ja|陶芸車輪の固定|zh|固定陶轮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fixing of the Potter's Wheel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”, which was celebrated in Esna simultaneously with the {{ill|Dendera|en|Dendera|WD=Q29019|lt=Dendera|1=Dendera}} Festival “{{ill|Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|de|Erheben des Himmels (Ptah)|ja|Ptahのための天の掲揚|zh|为Ptah举天|lt=Raising of the Sky for Ptah|lt_ja=Ptahのための天の掲揚|lt_de=Erheben des Himmels für Ptah|lt_zh=为Ptah举天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raising of the Sky (Ptah)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”. Reasons for this are found in the special significance of {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum|1=Khnum}} and {{ill|Neith|en|Neith|WD=Q191815|lt=Neith (Egyptian Mythology)|1=Neith}} in Esna, who were worshipped there as creator deities. The festival {{ill|symbolized|de|symbolisierte|ja|象徴した|zh|象征了|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/symbolized|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} the creation of Ancient Egypt, with the thematic complex including eight primordial gods, thirty additional gods, the births of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian Mythology)|1=Ra}}, {{ill|Apophis|en|Apophis|WD=Q190511|lt=Apophis|1=Apophis}}, as well as {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|WD=Q146921|lt=Thoth|1=Thoth}} and the creation words of the Neith-Methyer. The celebration began on the evening of the previous day with the procession to {{ill|Sais, Egypt|en|Sais, Egypt|WD=Q381038|lt=Sais|1=Sais, Egypt}}, where torches, candles, and other objects were lit: {{Zitat|Lighting of torches inside the temple. The men and women celebrate a beautiful day, the whole city rejoices, so that there is no sleep for anyone until dawn.|Excerpt from {{ill|Esna 207|de|Esna 207|ja|エスナ207|zh|埃斯纳207|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Esna 207|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna.'' Wiesbaden 2000, p. 87.}} {{ill|Herodotus|en|Herodotus|WD=Q26825|lt=Herodotus|1=Herodotus}} reports,Herodotus, ''Histories'' Book 2, 62. that the custom of lighting torches followed by the ensuing celebration was also practiced outside of Sais. It is therefore likely that this festival practice provided the festive framework everywhere. == Literature == * Maria-Theresia Derchain-Urtel: ''The Visual and Textual Design in Esna.'' In: {{ill|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|de|Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja|ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン＝ファン・エルスベルゲン|zh|乌苏拉·费霍文-范-埃尔斯贝尔根|lt=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_ja=ウルズラ・フェアホーヴェン|lt_de=Ursula Verhoeven|lt_zh=乌苏拉·费霍文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ursula Verhoeven-van Elsbergen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Erhart Graefe|de|Erhart Graefe|ja|エルハルト・グレーフェ|zh|埃尔哈特·格雷费|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Erhart Graefe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (eds.): ''Religion and Philosophy in Ancient Egypt. Festschrift for Philippe Derchain on his 65th birthday on 24 July 1991'' (= ''Orientalia Lovaniensia Analecta. (OLA).'' Volume 39). Department of Oriental Studies, among others, Leuven 1991, ISBN 90-6831-337-1, pp. 107–121. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーエン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Sky over Esna. A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt on the Example of the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna'' (= ''Egyptological Treatises. (ÄA).'' Volume 64). Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5 (Also: Tübingen, Univ., Master's thesis, 1998). == References ==
